commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=033a501892f19abeec5b061355d15f52897bf94a)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes the `sessions_yield` tool description to say it is subagent-only, cron completion does not resume the session, and cron waits should use `sessions_spawn`, `openclaw cron run --wait`, or `cron.runs`.

PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the changed description feeds generated Codex dynamic-tool snapshots, and current committed fixtures still contain the old description; I did not run the generator because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof and the linked issue closure decision need human handling; automation cannot supply real evidence from the contributor's setup or decide the product contract.

target.title: docs(tools): clarify sessions_yield is subagent-only, does not resume on cron completion
target.body:
Fixes #95535

## Motivation

The `sessions_yield` tool description does not clarify that it only works with subagents, not with isolated cron jobs. When users call `sessions_yield` to wait for a cron job to complete, the main session never resumes, leaving the user confused.

## Changes

- `src/agents/tools/sessions-yield-tool.ts` — Update tool description to clarify:
  - `sessions_yield` is subagent-only
  - Cron job completion does NOT resume the yielded session
  - Use `sessions_spawn` for cron workflows, or `openclaw cron run --wait` / `cron.runs` to poll cron completion via CLI

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions-yield-tool.test.ts` → 4/4 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` → 4/4 passed

## Compatibility

- Only the tool description string was changed; no runtime behavior was modified
- No breaking changes
- No migration needed

## CHANGELOG

No CHANGELOG entry needed — documentation-only fix.
